skill.name: doc-coauthoring
skill.description: Guides users through a structured workflow for drafting, refining, and reader-testing documentation, proposals, specifications, and decision documents.
skill.summary: Co-author structured documents through context gathering, iterative drafting, and reader testing.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and ordinary prose, not executable code or cryptographic use. The real risks are legitimate but sensitive workflow behaviors: reading shared documents or team channels through integrations, and creating or editing files and artifacts. Publish with a warning that users should confirm connector access and review file changes.

medium_findings.title: Connected Service Reads May Expose Sensitive Context
medium_findings.description: The workflow tells the assistant to use integrations to fetch shared documents, current document state, team channels, and document storage content. This is appropriate for co-authoring but can pull sensitive internal material into the session if users do not limit connector access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly instruct use of integrations for shared documents, messaging channels, and document storage. The behavior is legitimate, but the privacy risk is clear from the workflow context.

medium_findings.title: Workflow Creates and Edits Workspace Documents
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to create artifacts or markdown files and to edit document sections with replacement operations. This is normal for a writing workflow but can overwrite or alter user documents if filenames and edits are not reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file and artifact write instructions are explicit and repeated. They are not malicious, but they create a concrete filesystem modification risk.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged inline backtick text as Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines name assistant editing tools or example filenames in Markdown and do not contain shell commands, Ruby code, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell source. No command invocation syntax or interpolation path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at prose lines in the skill instructions. The cited locations describe documentation workflow steps and contain no cryptographic APIs, hash functions, encryption settings, or key handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line is natural-language workflow text. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: Manual Claude Link Is Benign but Involves User Sharing
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points users to Claude for manual reader testing and does not perform a network request by itself. The surrounding instructions ask users to paste or share document content, so users should avoid sending confidential material without approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is visible prose guidance to open Claude, not hidden exfiltration code. The only risk is user-mediated disclosure during manual testing.

content.user_title: Draft Better Documents With Reader Testing
content.value_statement: Important documents often fail because context stays in the author’s head. This skill guides structured context gathering, section drafting, refinement, and reader testing.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through context gathering before drafting starts.
Suggests document sections for specs, proposals, RFCs, and decision docs.
Builds documents section by section with user curation and feedback.
Creates markdown files or artifacts when the environment supports writing.
Uses reader testing to find ambiguity, missing context, and contradictions.
Supports final review for flow, redundancy, and factual checks.
limitations: It depends on the user to provide accurate source context and constraints.
Connected document or messaging access requires available integrations and user permission.
It does not verify external facts unless the environment provides reliable sources.
Reader testing with a separate Claude instance may require manual steps.

use_cases.title: Draft a Technical Spec
use_cases.description: Turn scattered architecture notes into a structured spec with sections for goals, constraints, trade-offs, and rollout details.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Decision Document
use_cases.description: Capture context, alternatives, rationale, and expected impact so reviewers can understand the decision without extra meetings.

use_cases.title: Improve Internal Documentation
use_cases.description: Refine existing docs, check assumptions, and test whether a new reader can answer practical questions from the document.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a first draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me co-author a technical document. Start by asking the context questions from the doc-coauthoring workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Notes Into a Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc-coauthoring workflow to turn these notes into a proposal. Ask clarifying questions before drafting sections.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for improving a document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft with the doc-coauthoring workflow. Identify gaps, suggest section improvements, and edit one section at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Run Reader Testing
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for validation before sharing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reader testing on this document. Predict reader questions, test whether the document answers them, and propose targeted fixes.

output_examples.input: I need to write an RFC for a database migration.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about audience, desired impact, constraints, existing templates, migration risks, and available source material before proposing a section outline.

output_examples.input: Here is my draft decision doc. It feels unclear.
output: A gap list for missing context.
Targeted edits for one section instead of a full rewrite.

output_examples.input: Test whether readers will understand this proposal.
output_examples.output: The assistant generates realistic reader questions, checks whether the document answers them, and lists concise fixes for ambiguous sections.

best_practices: Provide audience, goal, constraints, and source material before asking for a draft.
Approve file names and connected-source access before the assistant reads or writes content.
Use reader testing before sharing important documents with stakeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a polished final document before sharing enough context.
Do not connect broad team data sources unless the document needs them.
Do not accept reader testing results without checking facts and ownership yourself.

faq.question: What kinds of documents does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports documentation, proposals, technical specs, RFCs, decision docs, and other structured workplace writing.

faq.question: Does it write the whole document at once?
faq.answer: No. It works section by section so the user can guide priorities, style, and scope.

faq.question: Can it use existing documents or team channels?
faq.answer: Yes, if integrations are available and the user allows access. Users should limit access to relevant sources.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow can create markdown files or artifacts when the environment supports file creation.

faq.question: What is reader testing?
faq.answer: Reader testing checks whether a fresh reader can answer realistic questions using only the document.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for human review?
faq.answer: No. The skill helps improve clarity, but owners must verify facts, links, and final quality.
